title: missing en-CH and it-CH in LangCountryData and lang-country config file
author: mkocurek
body:
First of all: thanks for that really neat repo. Well done. Second, this is less an issue report more a request to add two languages to it.

So, here we go: can you add the following to the repo, pls.

1. add `'en-CH', 'it-CH',` to the vendor/stefro/laravel-lang-country/config/lang-country.php and
2. add the following two language files

- Swiss - Englisch (a lot of expats come each year to Switzerland) - next to german, english has become a mandatory language: vendor/stefro/laravel-lang-country/src/LangCountryData/en-CH.json
   `{
    "country": "CH",
    "country_name": "Switzerland",
    "country_name_local": "Switzerland",
    "lang": "en",
    "name": "Switzerland - English",
    "date_numbers": "d.m.Y",
    "date_numbers_full_capitals": "DD.MM.YYYY",
    "date_words_without_day": "j F Y",
    "date_words_with_day": "l j F Y",
    "date_birthday": "j F",
    "time_format": "H:i",
    "emoji_flag": "🇨🇭",
    "currency_code": "CHF",
    "currency_symbol": "₣",
    "currency_symbol_local": "S₣r",
    "currency_name": "Swiss Franc",
    "currency_name_local": "Swiss Franc"
  }
`

- besides de-CH and fr-CH we do speak Italian in Switzerland as well ... I know ;)
vendor/stefro/laravel-lang-country/src/LangCountryData/it-CH.json
  `{
    "country": "CH",
    "country_name": "Switzerland",
    "country_name_local": "Svizzera",
    "lang": "it",
    "name": "Svizzera - Italia",
    "date_numbers": "d.m.Y",
    "date_numbers_full_capitals": "DD.MM.YYYY",
    "date_words_without_day": "j F Y",
    "date_words_with_day": "l j F Y",
    "date_birthday": "j F",
    "time_format": "H:i",
    "emoji_flag": "🇨🇭",
    "currency_code": "CHF",
    "currency_symbol": "₣",
    "currency_symbol_local": "S₣r",
    "currency_name": "Swiss Franc",
    "currency_name_local": "Franco Svizzero"
  }
`

Thanks, Michael

title: Support for Laravel 12.x.

body:
Good day, please add support for Laravel 12.x.
Many thanks!

title: Your repository packagist may not working.
author: askaralimkndr

title: Override language file not found
author: rabol
body:
I have created a da-DK.json, and put it in the lang/lang-country-overrides folder, however I get this error:

```
file_get_contents(/Users/rabol/code/web/test/vendor/stefro/laravel-lang-country/src/LangCountryData/da-DK.json): Failed to open stream: No such file or directory
```

title: How to install when using Breeze or Jetstream as the installation instructions is not covering these
author: rabol
body:
<!-- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Detailed description

The installation instructions tell one to add some code to the ```app\Http\Controllers\Auth\LoginController.php``` which does not exists if you use Laravel Breeze ot Laravel Jetstream

Adding the middleware will make the app fail with this exception:

```
Exception
The lang_country session is not set
```


also the section with the middleware could need a update, I believe that ```LangCountrySession::class```should be added 
Not sure if this should be treated as a enhancement or a question.

## Context

I'm pretty sure may other users would benefit from updated documentation

## Possible implementation

Update the documentation to support Laravel Breeze and Laravel Jetstream

## Your environment

Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in and how to reproduce it.

* Version used PHP 8.2.11, Laravel 10.28
* Operating system and version (e.g. MacOS ventura):

title: Potential additional locale data source
author: drbyte
body:
Hi,
One package I've used in various applications, which provides a very comprehensive set of locale data, is https://github.com/mledoze/countries
Perhaps using its data may allow your package to expand its reach.

title: xx_YY json lang file not loaded
author: gabgren
body:
<!-- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Detailed description

Hi ! 
I'm building an app for en-US, en-CA and fr-CA regions/languages.

Generally speaking, en-CA and en-US could share the same en.json file, but there are a few times where i'll need them to be different. Reading your doc, and the issue #13 , I thought this would be easy, but that doesn't seem to work. Maybe i'm getting something wrong.

## Context

My current locale is fr-CA

I have a ressources/lang/fr-CA.json file that looks like this:
`{
    "en-CA": "Canada (English)",
    "fr-CA": "Canada (Français)",
    "en-US": "United States (English)"
}`

I created an override file in: ressources/lang/lang-country-overrides/fr-CA.json
in which I define "lang" as "fr-CA" instead of just "fr"
`{
  "country": "CA",
  "country_name": "Canada",
  "country_name_local": "Canada",
  "lang": "fr-CA",
  "name": "Canada (Français)",
  "date_numbers": "d/m/Y",
  "date_numbers_full_capitals": "DD/MM/YYYY",
  "date_words_without_day": "j F Y",
  "date_words_with_day": "l j F Y",
  "date_birthday": "j F",
  "time_format": "h:i a",
  "emoji_flag": "🇨🇦",
  "currency_code": "CAD",
  "currency_symbol": "$",
  "currency_symbol_local": "$CAD",
  "currency_name": "Canadian Dollar",
  "currency_name_local": "Dollars Canadien"
}`

In a component I call `{{ __($lang['lang_country']) }}`

This should look at ressources/lang/fr-CA.json for the current lang_country key, which is `fr-CA` and display `Canada (Français)`, but I only get `fr-CA`, so the translation did not happen.

If I rename ressources/lang/fr-CA.json to fr.json, it will work... So it seems, even if i've set lang in the override, that it's still looking for the fr.json file

I know the override file gets loaded because if I output `name`, I get `Canada (Français)` instead of the default name.
(I know I can use this as a workaround for what I originally intended to do, but the problem still persists and I'll still have a problem later when I want a different translation between en-CA and en-US)

So the override file seems to work, but then it fails to load the proper lang json file.

## environment
This is Laravel 8 and this lib's latest release, 2.4.1


Thanks for your help :)

title: lang_country not stored in DB as expected
author: jayenne
body:
Hello,
I have followed the instructions to install this package and expected the lang_country to be stored in the new user.lang_country table of my database but it remains NULL even after numerous logins and cache dumps etc.

Should it be added to the db or do I need to add it manually on login somehow?

title: date example in readme is incirrect.
author: jayenne
body:
Hi,
the example date in the readme file is incrrect. I don't know if this is a typo of something that reflects the code too...

you have: jan 1th 2018; 1 jan 2018

it should be: jan 1st 2018; 1st jan 2018
--also: you would only really drop the st/nd/rd/th if the month is a number too.

title: en-GB date format incorrect
author: davidphtee
body:
Loving this but, as a Brit we always use day-month-year for our dates. It should really be like en-AU. More at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Date_format_by_country

{
  "country": "GB",
  "lang": "en",
  "name": "English",
  "date_numbers": "d/m/Y",
  "date_numbers_full_capitals": "DD/MM/YYYY",
  "date_words_without_day": "j F Y",
  "date_words_with_day": "l j F Y",
  "date_birthday": "j F",
  "time_format": "h:i a",
  "emoji_flag": "🇬🇧"
}

title: wrong ressource/lang loaded...
author: FrancoisDucobu
body:
Hello,

I live in Belgium and I develop an application in french, dutch and I think about trying to sell this application in France.
Some words are different in French Belgian and French France...
In Belgium, the administration is called "Administration Communale" in French Belgian
And called "Mairie" in French France

I found your package.
It is very usefull !

But I have an issue and I think there's something I didn't understand correctly.

So in my ressource/lang, I have 3 folders (nl-BE, fr-BE and fr-FR)
In my session variable, i got 'lang' => 'fr', and 'lang_country' => 'fr-BE'
But the application load the 'fr' language...

In the README file, you wrote :
```
There will ALWAYS be set three sessions:
lang_country (example: nl-BE)
locale (examples: nl, es-CO)
locale_for_date (example: nl)
```
So I thought that locale would contain "fr-BE" instead of "fr"

here is my config/lang-country.php

```
return [

    'fallback' => 'fr-BE',

    'allowed' => [
        'nl-BE',
        'fr-BE',
        'fr-FR',
    ],

    'lang_switcher_middleware' => ['web'],

];
```

Did I miss something ?

Thanks for your help.

Francois
